decisions.title: Update Time Fields for Clarity and User Experience
decisions.summary: The decision was made to enhance the time fields in the Chef-AI platform to provide a more accurate and user-friendly experience. This involved introducing new time categories and migrating existing data to the new structure.

decisions.title: Implement Separate Time Displays for Clarity
decisions.summary: To improve clarity, the UI was updated to display prep time, cook time, and total time separately, making it easier for users to understand the time commitment for each recipe.

decisions.title: Enhance Search Functionality with Time Filters
decisions.summary: The search feature was enhanced to allow users to filter recipes based on prep time, cook time, or total time, providing a more tailored and efficient search experience.

decisions.title: Integrate New Time Fields into API Endpoints
decisions.summary: API updates were made to include the new time fields for all recipe endpoints, ensuring consistency and compatibility across the platform.

decisions.title: Conduct Beta Testing and Gather User Feedback
decisions.summary: A beta testing phase was implemented with 1,000 users to gather feedback on the new time display. Survey results and support ticket data were analyzed to refine the time structure and user experience.

decisions.title: Implement Future Enhancements for Time-Based Features
decisions.summary: Future enhancements were outlined, including smart scheduling with calendar integration, batch cooking optimization, time tracking for user-reported cooking times, and AI predictions for more accurate time estimates.

decisions.title: Superior Data Sources
decisions.summary: The AI system analyzes the entire recipe, understands chemical interactions, respects traditional methods, and considers nutritional impact.

decisions.title: Smart Substitution Logic
decisions.summary: Replaces ingredients based on function, maintains flavor and texture, and adapts to cooking conditions.

decisions.title: User Benefits
decisions.summary: High success rate, taste preservation, dietary safety, and cost optimization.

decisions.title: Real-World Examples
decisions.summary: Demonstrates understanding of baking, sauces, spices, and substitutions like coconut milk in curries.

decisions.title: Business Impact
decisions.summary: Builds user trust, improves recipe success, justifies premium pricing, and differentiates the product.

decisions.title: Technical Foundation
decisions.summary: Trained on a large dataset, validated by experts, improved with user feedback, and supported by research partnerships.

decisions.title: Future Roadmap
decisions.summary: Plans to incorporate allergy intelligence, cost optimization, seasonal adaptation, and personalization.

decisions.title: Target Audience Segmentation
decisions.summary: Identify and prioritize user personas based on demographics, pain points, and goals.

decisions.title: Product Strategy Focus
decisions.summary: Develop core features targeting busy parents, social features for millennials, professional tools for chefs, and educational content for beginner cooks.

decisions.title: Business Prioritization
decisions.summary: Prioritize user segments based on market potential and long-term value: busy parents, health-conscious millennials, professional chefs, and beginner cooks.

decisions.title: Smart Shopping Lists
decisions.summary: Generate shopping lists, integrate pantry, map store layouts, track budgets, and compare prices across retailers.

decisions.title: AI Meal Planning
decisions.summary: Generate weekly meal schedules, customize for dietary preferences, prioritize seasonal ingredients, optimize leftovers, and coordinate family meals.

decisions.title: Smart Features Rollout
decisions.summary: Roll out features in Q1-Q4 2024: basic shopping list, store price comparisons, AI meal planning, and family coordination.

decisions.title: User Value Proposition
decisions.summary: Save time, reduce costs, relieve stress, and support health goals by automating meal planning and shopping.

decisions.title: Business Impact
decisions.summary: Increase user engagement, retention, and revenue through premium features and grocery store partnerships.

decisions.title: Success Metrics
decisions.summary: Measure success through shopping list usage, meal plan adoption, cost savings, and time savings.

decisions.title: Quick-View Hover System
decisions.summary: Implement a hover system for recipe cards with a fade-in animation and key recipe details.

decisions.title: Hover State Implementation
decisions.summary: Design the hover state with subtle card lift, overlay, and action buttons.

decisions.title: Mobile-First Touch Interactions
decisions.summary: Optimize for mobile with touch targets, long press, and swipe gestures.

decisions.title: Responsive Behavior
decisions.summary: Ensure responsiveness across devices: desktop, tablet, and mobile.

decisions.title: Animation Performance
decisions.summary: Enhance animation performance with GPU acceleration and lazy loading.

decisions.title: Accessibility Features
decisions.summary: Implement keyboard navigation, screen reader support, and focus management for accessibility.

decisions.title: Component States
decisions.summary: Define component states: default, hover, active, and disabled.

decisions.title: Direction Attribute for Hebrew Pages
decisions.summary: Set `dir="rtl"` on the HTML element for Hebrew pages to ensure the correct text direction.

decisions.title: CSS Logical Properties for Margin
decisions.summary: Use `margin-inline-start` instead of `margin-left` to handle margins logically for RTL layouts.

decisions.title: Flexbox Direction for RTL
decisions.summary: Set `flex-direction: row-reverse` to reverse the direction of flex items in RTL layouts.

decisions.title: Grid Positioning for RTL
decisions.summary: Reverse the order of columns/rows automatically to ensure proper positioning in RTL grids.

decisions.title: Text Alignment for Hebrew Content
decisions.summary: Set `text-align: right` for all Hebrew content to align text properly.

decisions.title: Handling Mixed Content
decisions.summary: Use `unicode-bidi: plaintext` to ensure proper handling of mixed content (e.g., Hebrew and Latin) in terms of text direction.

decisions.title: Direction for Numbers and Latin within RTL Text
decisions.summary: Maintain the LTR direction for numbers and Latin characters within RTL text.

decisions.title: Auto-positioning for Quotes and Punctuation
decisions.summary: Allow quotes and punctuation to auto-position based on the text direction (RTL or LTR).

decisions.title: Breadcrumb Arrows Direction in RTL
decisions.summary: Point breadcrumb arrows to the left instead of the right in RTL navigation.

decisions.title: Hamburger Menu Icon Flip
decisions.summary: Flip the hamburger menu icon to open from the right side in RTL layouts.

decisions.title: Pagination Button Swap
decisions.summary: Swap the positions of previous/next buttons in RTL pagination.

decisions.title: Progress Bar Filling Direction
decisions.summary: Fill progress bars from right to left in RTL contexts.

decisions.title: Search Input Magnifying Glass Position
decisions.summary: Place the magnifying glass icon on the right side of the search input field.

decisions.title: Dropdown Menus Opening Direction
decisions.summary: Make dropdown menus open to the left instead of the right in RTL contexts.

decisions.title: Modal Dialogs Slide-in Direction
decisions.summary: Slide modal dialogs in from the right side to match the RTL layout.

decisions.title: Tooltips Positioning
decisions.summary: Position tooltips to the left of the trigger element in RTL contexts.

decisions.title: Font Selection for RTL
decisions.summary: Choose fonts optimized for RTL languages, such as 'Assistant' or 'Rubik'.

decisions.title: Line Height Adjustment for Hebrew Readability
decisions.summary: Slightly increase the line height to improve the readability of Hebrew text.

decisions.title: Letter Spacing for Hebrew Text Flow
decisions.summary: Reduce letter spacing to enhance the flow of Hebrew text.

decisions.title: Word Breaking for Hebrew Words
decisions.summary: Ensure proper word breaking for Hebrew words to maintain readability.

decisions.title: RTL Rules Application at All Breakpoints
decisions.summary: Apply RTL rules consistently across all breakpoints, including mobile-first designs.

decisions.title: Reversed Swipe Gestures for RTL
decisions.summary: Reverse swipe gestures for touch interactions in RTL contexts.

decisions.title: Tab Order Respecting RTL Layout
decisions.summary: Ensure the tab order respects the RTL layout for keyboard navigation.

decisions.title: Focus Management for RTL
decisions.summary: Display focus rings on the right side to match the RTL layout.

decisions.title: Primary Colors
decisions.summary: Define the primary colors for the Chef-AI Frontend: Chef-Red, Kitchen-White, Shadow-Gray, and Light-Gray.

decisions.title: Typography System
decisions.summary: Establish the typography system with font weights, sizes, and line heights for headings, body text, recipe titles, and ingredient lists.

decisions.title: Readability Rules
decisions.summary: Set rules for contrast ratio, font sizes, line height, and character limit to ensure readability and WCAG AA compliance.

decisions.title: Kitchen Environment Optimization
decisions.summary: Optimize for kitchen environments with large touch targets, high contrast, bold typography, and simple icons.

decisions.title: Responsive Typography
decisions.summary: Define responsive typography scales for different screen sizes: mobile, tablet, desktop, and large screens.

decisions.title: Component Styling
decisions.summary: Style components like buttons, cards, inputs, and navigation with specific colors, shadows, and focus states.

decisions.title: Select Gemini-1.5-Flash AI Model
decisions.summary: Choose the Gemini-1.5-Flash model for its speed, cost-effectiveness, and accuracy in recipe analysis.

decisions.title: Performance Trade-offs
decisions.summary: Prioritize speed, cost efficiency, and acceptable accuracy for recipe analysis during the startup phase.

decisions.title: Recipe Analysis Tasks
decisions.summary: Define tasks: ingredient extraction, instruction parsing, dietary classification, and difficulty assessment.

decisions.title: Model Configuration
decisions.summary: Set temperature to 0.3, max tokens to 2048, and include system prompts for food safety and accuracy.

decisions.title: Rate Limiting
decisions.summary: Limit users to 100 requests per minute to manage performance and costs.

decisions.title: Cost Analysis
decisions.summary: Compare costs: Flash model ($0.00015/1000 tokens) vs Pro model ($0.00075/1000 tokens) for 5M analyses.

decisions.title: Quality Assurance
decisions.summary: Implement human review, feedback loop, A/B testing, and a fallback system for quality control.

decisions.title: Future Considerations
decisions.summary: Plan to upgrade to Gemini-2.0, fine-tune with recipe-specific data, handle complex recipes, and monitor performance.

decisions.title: Gluten-Free Validation
decisions.summary: Ensure gluten-free validation by maintaining an extensive ingredient database, flagging cross-contamination, verifying certifications, and detecting hidden gluten sources.

decisions.title: Nut-Free Validation
decisions.summary: Validate nut-free recipes by maintaining a nut family database, issuing processing alerts, identifying nut oils, and verifying the safety of nut-free alternatives.

decisions.title: Safety Logic Framework
decisions.summary: Implement a safety logic framework with zero tolerance, a conservative approach, multi-layer checks, and user override for risk acceptance.

decisions.title: Validation Process
decisions.summary: Utilize AI analysis, database cross-checks, human review by nutritionists, and user warnings to ensure a robust validation process.

decisions.title: Risk Categories
decisions.summary: Categorize risks as HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, or UNKNOWN based on the presence of allergens, cross-contamination, trace amounts, and data availability.

decisions.title: Emergency Protocols
decisions.summary: Establish emergency protocols including immediate removal of unsafe recipes, user notifications, health advisories, and legal compliance with allergen labeling requirements.

decisions.title: Liability Protection
decisions.summary: Implement liability protection measures such as disclaimers, user agreements, expert consultations, and insurance coverage for allergen incidents.

decisions.title: Continuous Monitoring
decisions.summary: Continuously monitor and improve allergen safety through user reports, database updates, regulatory changes tracking, and an audit trail of safety decisions.

decisions.title: Content Integrity and Safety Measures
decisions.summary: A comprehensive approach to ensuring recipe safety, including toxicity detection, temperature control, allergen management, and nutritional balance checks.

decisions.title: Multi-Layered Safety Validation
decisions.summary: Utilizes AI analysis, database cross-referencing, expert review, and user feedback to validate recipe safety.

decisions.title: Critical Safety Checks
decisions.summary: Focuses on raw meat, cross-contamination, expiration dates, and dietary restrictions to prevent foodborne illnesses and allergic reactions.

decisions.title: Unsafe Content Detection
decisions.summary: Identifies harmful ingredient combinations, incorrect temperatures, toxic ingredients, and life-threatening allergens.

decisions.title: Verification and Emergency Response
decisions.summary: Implements automated scanning, manual review, community moderation, and expert consultation for safety verification. Includes immediate removal of unsafe recipes, user notifications, health warnings, and regulatory reporting.

decisions.title: Quality Assurance and Legal Compliance
decisions.summary: Ensures recipe testing, accuracy metrics, false positive rate minimization, and continuous improvement. Adheres to food safety standards, liability protection, expert documentation, and insurance coverage.

decisions.title: Family Secret Recipe Protection
decisions.summary: Protecting family recipes with encryption, private storage, and access control.

decisions.title: User Encryption Standards
decisions.summary: Ensuring data security at rest, in transit, and end-to-end with encryption and TLS.

decisions.title: Privacy Controls
decisions.summary: Offering users control over data visibility, portability, deletion, and analytics.

decisions.title: Sensitive Information Handling
decisions.summary: Enhanced protection for sensitive data like family recipes, dietary restrictions, and personal preferences.

decisions.title: Compliance Framework
decisions.summary: Adhering to GDPR, CCPA, and data minimization principles.

decisions.title: Security Measures
decisions.summary: Implementing multi-factor authentication, session management, audit logging, and breach detection.

decisions.title: Third-Party Protection
decisions.summary: Securing API, partner integrations, and data sharing with explicit user consent and privacy clauses.

decisions.title: User Rights
decisions.summary: Empowering users with access, correction, portability, and objection rights over their data.

decisions.title: RESTful API Design
decisions.summary: The API follows RESTful design principles, utilizing standard HTTP methods and predictable patterns for easy caching and development.

decisions.title: User-Specific Data Handling
decisions.summary: User favorites and related data are separated from core recipe management endpoints to allow for different caching strategies and user-specific personalization.

decisions.title: Dedicated Search Endpoints
decisions.summary: Search functionality is provided through dedicated endpoints to accommodate specialized indexing and ranking requirements.

decisions.title: JWT Authentication Choice
decisions.summary: JWT (JSON Web Token) authentication is chosen for its stateless nature, scalability, mobile-friendliness, and performance benefits.

decisions.title: Token Structure and Security
decisions.summary: The token structure includes user ID, role, expiration, and refresh tokens. Security measures such as password hashing, token revocation, rate limiting, and CORS protection are implemented.

decisions.title: API Performance Optimization
decisions.summary: Performance features like pagination, field selection, compression, and URL-based versioning are implemented to enhance API performance and user experience.

decisions.title: Use MongoDB for Recipe Data
decisions.summary: MongoDB's flexible schema and document model are well-suited for nested recipe data, offering scalability and efficient indexing for ingredients, cuisine, and cooking time. Its JSON integration allows for direct API responses without transformation.

decisions.title: Ingredient Scaling Strategies
decisions.summary: Different scaling strategies are employed for various ingredient types: linear scaling for main ingredients, sublinear scaling for leavening agents and spices, and threshold scaling for critical flavorings and preservatives.

decisions.title: Performance Optimization
decisions.summary: Performance considerations include pre-computed tables for common serving multiples, density storage for accurate unit conversions, compound indexes for fast queries, and aggregation pipelines for server-side calculations to reduce client load.

decisions.title: Redis Search Acceleration
decisions.summary: Utilize Redis for recipe searches due to its in-memory speed, data structure support, persistence, and scalability.

decisions.title: Search Result Caching
decisions.summary: Implement query hashing, result storage, index caching, and autocomplete caching to optimize search performance.

decisions.title: Cache Layers
decisions.summary: Establish multiple cache layers with varying TTLs for different types of data: search results, recipe details, user preferences, and scaling calculations.

decisions.title: 24-Hour TTL Policy
decisions.summary: Set a 24-hour TTL for scaling calculations based on recipe stability, compute intensity, user patterns, and storage efficiency considerations.

decisions.title: TTL Strategy by Data Type
decisions.summary: Define TTL strategies for different data types: search results, recipe details, user sessions, and scaling data.

decisions.title: Cache Invalidation
decisions.summary: Implement time-based, event-driven, tag-based, and memory management strategies for cache invalidation to ensure data freshness and efficiency.

decisions.title: Performance Impact
decisions.summary: Achieve significant speed improvements and memory optimization through Redis caching, resulting in faster response times and efficient memory usage.

decisions.title: Storage Strategy for Food Photos
decisions.summary: Utilize AWS S3 for recipe image storage due to its durability, scalability, performance, and cost-effectiveness.

decisions.title: Bucket Organization
decisions.summary: Organize images in S3 buckets: original high-res, compressed versions, thumbnails, and a temporary user uploads area.

decisions.title: Image Compression with Sharp
decisions.summary: Use Sharp for image processing due to its performance, memory efficiency, format support, and quality control.

decisions.title: Compression Pipeline
decisions.summary: Implement a pipeline: upload, analyze, resize, compress, and store optimized images with metadata.

decisions.title: Compression Targets
decisions.summary: Set quality and size targets for main images, step photos, thumbnails, and WebP format.

decisions.title: Performance Optimization
decisions.summary: Implement a multi-format strategy with WebP as primary and JPEG as fallback. Use progressive loading and responsive images.

decisions.title: CDN Integration
decisions.summary: Integrate CloudFront for global distribution, set cache headers, enable Gzip compression, and use signed URLs for access control.

decisions.title: Cost Management
decisions.summary: Manage costs with intelligent tiering, lifecycle policies to archive old images, and achieve significant size reduction.

decisions.title: Quality Assurance
decisions.summary: Ensure food photos meet standards, maintain visual appeal, and optimize formats for each use case.

For tablets, use a base font size of 18px and a scale ratio of 1.25.
On desktops, set a base font size of 20px and a scale ratio of 1.25.
For large screens, use a base font size of 22px and a scale ratio of 1.125.

Toxicity Detection
rules.scope: Identify harmful ingredient combinations to prevent toxic reactions.

rules.rule: Temperature Safety
rules.scope: Validate cooking temperatures to ensure food safety and prevent undercooking.

rules.rule: Allergen Cross-Check
rules.scope: Ensure accurate allergen labeling to protect users with allergies.

rules.rule: Nutrition Balance
rules.scope: Detect nutritional deficiencies to prevent health risks.

rules.rule: AI Analysis
rules.scope: Gemini scans recipes for safety red flags, such as potential allergens or toxic ingredients.

rules.rule: Database Cross-Reference
rules.scope: Compare recipes against a database of safe recipe patterns to identify potential issues.

rules.rule: Expert Review
rules.scope: Professional chefs validate new recipes to ensure safety and quality.

rules.rule: User Feedback
rules.scope: Community reporting system allows users to flag safety issues and provide feedback.

rules.rule: Raw Meat Safety
rules.scope: Ensure proper cooking temperatures for raw meat to prevent foodborne illnesses.

rules.rule: Cross-Contamination
rules.scope: Flag unsafe food handling practices to prevent cross-contamination.

rules.rule: Expiration Dates
rules.scope: Warn users about potentially unsafe ingredients due to expiration dates.

rules.rule: Dietary Restrictions
rules.scope: Validate medical safety claims and accommodate dietary restrictions.

rules.rule: Harmful Combinations
rules.scope: Identify dangerous ingredient pairings to prevent adverse reactions.

rules.rule: Incorrect Temperatures
rules.scope: Flag undercooked food to prevent foodborne illnesses.

rules.rule: Toxic Ingredients
rules.scope: Identify poisonous plants or substances to prevent accidental ingestion.

rules.rule: Allergic Reactions
rules.scope: Detect life-threatening allergens to protect users with severe allergies.

rules.rule: Automated Scanning
rules.scope: Real-time safety analysis during recipe creation to identify potential issues early on.

rules.rule: Manual Review
rules.scope: Human verification for high-risk recipes to ensure accuracy and safety.

rules.rule: Community Moderation
rules.scope: User flagging system for safety issues, allowing the community to contribute to safety checks.

rules.rule: Expert Consultation
rules.scope: Nutritionist review for medical claims to ensure accuracy and safety.

rules.rule: Immediate Removal
rules.scope: Unsafe recipes are removed within 30 minutes to protect users.

rules.rule: User Notification
rules.scope: Alert system to notify affected users about unsafe recipes.

rules.rule: Health Warnings
rules.scope: Clear safety disclaimers are provided when needed to inform users about potential risks.

rules.rule: Regulatory Reporting
rules.scope: Compliance with food safety authorities to ensure legal and ethical standards are met.

rules.rule: Recipe Testing
rules.scope: Physical testing of AI-generated recipes to ensure safety and quality.

rules.rule: Accuracy Metrics
rules.scope: Track safety prediction accuracy to improve the system's performance.

rules.rule: False Positive Rate
rules.scope: Minimize incorrect safety warnings to reduce user confusion and frustration.

rules.rule: Continuous Improvement
rules.scope: Machine learning from safety outcomes to enhance the system's ability to identify potential issues.

rules.rule: Food Safety Standards
rules.scope: Adhere to FDA and international regulations to ensure legal compliance.

rules.rule: Liability Protection
rules.scope: Clear disclaimers and user agreements to protect against liability.

rules.rule: Expert Documentation
rules.scope: Maintain professional validation records for legal and ethical purposes.

rules.rule: Insurance Coverage
rules.scope: Product liability insurance to cover recipe safety-related incidents.

Use MongoDB for its flexible schema and document model to handle varying recipe structures.
Implement linear scaling for main ingredients to maintain proportional relationships.
Apply sublinear scaling for leavening agents and spices to prevent over-seasoning in large batches.
Use threshold scaling for critical flavorings and preservatives to maintain minimum and maximum amounts.
Pre-compute tables for common serving multiples to optimize performance.
Store ingredient densities for accurate unit conversions.
Utilize compound indexes for fast queries on ingredients and amounts.
Implement aggregation pipelines for server-side calculations to reduce client load.
Use Redis for recipe searches due to its speed and data structure support.
Implement caching for search results, recipe details, user preferences, and scaling calculations with appropriate TTLs.
Set TTLs based on data type and characteristics: search results, recipe details, user sessions, and scaling data.
Employ time-based, event-driven, tag-based, and memory management techniques for cache invalidation.
Optimize performance by leveraging Redis caching, resulting in faster response times and efficient memory usage.
Use AWS S3 for recipe image storage due to its durability, scalability, performance, and cost-effectiveness.
Organize images in S3 buckets: original high-res, compressed versions, thumbnails, and a temporary user uploads area.
Use Sharp for image processing due to its performance, memory efficiency, format support, and quality control.
Implement a pipeline: upload, analyze, resize, compress, and store optimized images with metadata.
Set quality and size targets for main images, step photos, thumbnails, and WebP format.
Implement a multi-format strategy with WebP as primary and JPEG as fallback. Use progressive loading and responsive images.
Integrate CloudFront for global distribution, set cache headers, enable Gzip compression, and use signed URLs for access control.
Manage costs with intelligent tiering, lifecycle policies to archive old images, and achieve significant size reduction.
Ensure food photos meet standards, maintain visual appeal, and optimize formats for each use case.
